content: Verified Mode: A temporary elevated state (lasting 60 minutes) achieved after a BCCC, allowing for Tier-3 movements.
Recovery Mode: A restricted state following the loss of a FIDO2 token, requiring a notarized affidavit and a 72-hour cooling-off period.
Hard-Hold: A non-negotiable, system-enforced 10-business-day hold placed on inbound funds from non-whitelisted institutions.
Dormant Mode: A system state triggered after 120 days of zero activity, requiring a physical BCCC to reactivate.
Clean Room Device: A hardware-fingerprinted computer or mobile device that has completed the 48-hour Enrollment Circuit and is free of remote-access software.
Active Liquidity: The portion of the account balance held in the Flagship Cash Account (BC-PROD-001) that is not currently pledged as collateral.
A-Side / B-Side Control: A dual-authorization requirement where two separate individuals (e.g., Client and Co-Signer, or Banker and Manager) must provide distinct cryptographic signatures.
Art and Antiquities: Permitted only with a 3rd party appraisal and a 'Provenance Certificate' for every transaction exceeding $1M.
Concentrated Stock: Lower ILV (20-40%) for holdings where a single ticker exceeds 20% of the total portfolio value.
Travel Registry: Pre-registered international travel for up to 21 days (Requires filing 48 hours prior to departure).
Manual Opt-Out: Clients may choose to hold all funds locally at Sun Chip, acknowledging that balances >$250k are uninsured.
Fee Waiver: Only if Policy BC-SLA-003 (Service Level Agreement) was breached in the prior 30 days.
Intra-Family Funding: Temporary drops are permitted for up to 5 business days to fund a new Sun Chip account for a first-degree relative.
Payroll Exception: Higher outbound ACH limits for entity accounts with verified payroll providers (Requires 3 months of payroll history).
High Liquidity Offset: DTI may be expanded to 45% if the client holds 60+ months of PITI reserves at Sun Chip.
Impaired Speech: For clients with documented medical conditions (Requires pre-filed ADA accommodation form and a court-appointed Power of Attorney).
Immediate Release: For Tier-1 Whitelisted recipients (Private Schools, Tax Authorities, Verified Law Firms).
Emergency Liquidation: Permitted only for documented 'Force Majeure' events, subject to a 2% 'Disruption Fee.'
Public Executive: SOW verification may be waived for C-suite officers of S&P 500 companies whose compensation is publicly filed with the SEC (Form 4).
Market Volatility Buffer: If the DAB drop is due to a 10% or greater market correction in a single week, the Cure Window is extended to 60 days.
Emergency Key Override: If the client is in a life-safety situation (Requires a 3rd party police report and Senior Risk Officer approval).
Diplomatic Grace: For clients stationed abroad on official U.S. government business (Requires SF-50 or equivalent).
R2: Step 1: The Client must display their Primary FIDO2 key to the camera for visual serial number verification.
R3: Step 2: The Banker provides a 'Challenge String' of five random numbers.
R4: Step 4: The Client responds with the 'Response Code' generated by their second FIDO2 key or a secure physical 'Paper Token.'
R5: Step 5: The Client must read the daily 'Phonetic Challenge Letters' from their physical Biscuit card.
R6: Step 6: The Client verbally confirms the exact dollar amount and the last 4 digits of the recipient's account number.
R7: Any failure in verbal clarity or background noise (suggesting coaching or duress) results in an immediate 'Duress Restricted Mode.'
R1: All wires exceeding $2,000,000.00 require a BCCC and a 4-hour 'Transaction Cooling-Off' unless the recipient is Tier-1 Whitelisted for 180+ days.
R2: Wires to new recipients are capped at $250,000.00 for the first 72 hours following whitelist approval.
R3: Wires cannot be initiated from non-trusted devices; 'Read Only' access applies to all non-verified hardware.
R4: 'Third-Party Escrow' wires require a signed 'Closing Statement' or 'Purchase Agreement' upload prior to initiation.
R5: Any wire to a 'Restricted Industry' (e.g., Crypto, Cannabis, Offshore Gaming) is automatically blocked and triggers a SAR review.
R6: Same-day wires initiated after 1:00 PM ET are processed on the next business day unless an 'Emergency After-Hours Fee' is paid.
R7: Sun Chip will never send wiring instructions via email; all instructions must be provided through the secure portal.
R1: Outbound ACH is capped at $100,000.00 per day per account. Amounts exceeding this must be sent via Fedwire.
R2: Inbound ACH transfers exceeding $500,000.00 are subject to a mandatory 5-day 'Settlement Hold'.
R3: Any ACH return with code R05, R07, or R10 (Unauthorized/Revoked) triggers an immediate 'Account Security Audit' and 48-hour Restricted Mode.
R4: Name mismatch (where the 'Receiver Name' on the ACH file does not match the Sun Chip account title) results in an automatic R03 return.
R5: No inbound ACH debits (pulls) are permitted by third parties unless the third party is on the 'Approved Debit Whitelist.'
R6: International ACH (IAT) transfers are strictly prohibited and will be returned with code R81.

R1: Sun Chip Private Bank does not provide services to the following Prohibited Industries: Cryptocurrency Exchanges, Custodians, or Mining Operations; Cannabis-Related Businesses (CRBs), including CBD/Hemp; Offshore Online Gambling and Gaming; Adult Entertainment and Related Infrastructure; Arms Dealing (Unlicensed) and Private Military Contractors; Political Action Committees (PACs) – Unless Tier-4 verified.
R2: Any outbound transaction to a known crypto-fiat gateway (e.g., Coinbase, Kraken) is automatically blocked.
R3: Business accounts must provide a 'Merchant Category Code' (MCC) audit annually to ensure no pivot into prohibited sectors.
R4: Funds derived from 'Shell Company' dividends are subject to Tier-2 SOW-Enhanced verification.
R5: Clients found attempting to 'Layer' funds through multiple LLCs to hide prohibited origins will face immediate account closure.
R1: All initial deposits must be accompanied by a 'Sun Chip SOW Declaration' form, identifying the primary origin of the $10M+ balance.
R2: For wealth derived from 'Business Sale,' the client must provide a signed Purchase Agreement and a 'Flow of Funds' statement from the closing agent.
R3: For wealth derived from 'Inheritance,' the client must provide a certified copy of the Will or Trust and a distribution letter from the Estate Executor.
R4: Inbound wires from non-U.S. banks (where allowed by exception) require 'MT799 Pre-Advice' and full SOW-Enhanced documentation.
R5: Any discrepancy between 'Declared SOW' and 'Observed Transaction Patterns' triggers an immediate 10-day 'Compliance Hold'.
R6: Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) are subject to mandatory SOW-Enhanced verification regardless of transaction size.
R1: All uninvested cash is automatically 'Swept' at 4:00 PM ET daily into the Insured Network.
R2: Clients may 'Exclude' up to 10 specific banks from the network to avoid exceeding FDIC limits if they hold direct accounts there.
R3: Yield is calculated based on the 'Network Weighted Average' and credited on the first business day of each month.
R4: A minimum 'Operational Buffer' of $50,000.00 remains in the local DDA for immediate debit availability.
R5: Sweep withdrawals for outbound wires are processed in real-time, but partner banks reserve the right to a 7-day delay.
R1: The minimum investment to activate the Treasury Ladder module is $5,000,000.00.
R2: Execution Window: All trades are placed at 10:00 AM ET on Tuesdays and Thursdays.
R3: Maturity Ranges: Clients may select 0-12 month (Short) or 0-5 year (Intermediate) ladders.
R4: 'Hold to Maturity': Sun Chip does not permit the early sale of Treasuries within this module; liquidity must come from the SBLOC.
R5: Reinvestment Rule: Maturing principal is rolled into the longest rung unless a 'Liquidity Draw' notice is filed 15 days prior.
R1: Proceeds must NOT be used to purchase more securities or pay off margin debt (Regulation U compliance).
R2: If the LTV exceeds the MLV, the client has exactly 24 hours to 'Cure' by depositing cash or pledging more securities.
R3: 'Automatic Liquidation': If the LTV exceeds 90% for equities, the system executes market sell orders immediately without notice.
R4: Interest rates are variable, set at SOFR plus a spread determined by the total AUM.
R5: The initial draw requires a BCCC and a notarized 'Pledge Agreement.'
R1: Minimum down payment is 20% of the purchase price.
R1: Prospective clients must demonstrate a minimum of $10,000,000.00 in liquid, unencumbered U.S. assets during the onboarding phase.
R3: 'Reserve Tiers': Clients must maintain significant PITI reserves in their Sun Chip cash account post-closing.
R4: Debt-to-Income (DTI) ratio is capped at 43%.
R5: Two independent appraisals are required for any property exceeding $3,000,000.00.
R2: Minimum FICO score of 740 is required for all signers.
R2: Assets held in real estate, private equity, jewelry, or cryptocurrency are excluded from the $10M minimum calculation.
R3: If the DAB falls below $10,000,000.00, the account enters 'Warning Status' at 12:01 AM ET the following day.
R4: A 30-day 'Cure Window' begins immediately upon entering Warning Status.
R5: During the Cure Window, the client must deposit sufficient funds to bring the DAB back to $10M+.
R6: Failure to restore the balance by the close of the Cure Window triggers the 'Automatic Downgrade Circuit.'
R7: Downgraded accounts lose access to the BCCC-privileged Service Circuits and are subject to a $5,000.00 monthly 'Sub-Tier Maintenance Fee.'
R8: Accounts remaining in Warning Status for two consecutive quarters are subject to 'Forced Closure Protocol'.
R1: All individual clients must possess a valid U.S. Social Security Number (SSN) and a current U.S. Passport or State-issued ID.
R2: Entity accounts (LLCs, Trusts, Corporations) must be incorporated in one of the 50 U.S. states or the District of Columbia.
R3: Any login attempt from an IP address outside the U.S. for more than 48 consecutive hours triggers 'International Anomaly Mode.'
R4: Clients in International Anomaly Mode are placed in Restricted Mode (no money movement) until a 'Live Video Residency Affirmation' is completed.
R5: The use of a VPN or Proxy to mask a non-U.S. location is classified as a 'Tier-1 Compliance Violation' and grounds for immediate account termination.
R6: Annual re-verification is required every January via submission of a primary utility bill (water, power, or gas) dated within the last 30 days.
R7: Beneficiaries of trusts must also meet U.S. residency requirements if they hold more than 10% of the beneficial interest.
R1: Sun Chip Private Bank does not support passwords, SMS authentication, or software-based TOTP (e.g., Google Authenticator).
R2: Every account must have exactly two FIDO2 keys registered: one 'Primary' and one 'Vaulted Backup.'
R3: New device enrollment requires a 48-hour 'Silent Cooling-Off' period before any outbound money movement is permitted.
R4: The removal or replacement of a FIDO2 key triggers a mandatory 72-hour 'Global Freeze' on all outbound transfers.
R5: FIDO2 keys must be physically inserted or tapped for every session; session persistence is limited to 15 minutes of inactivity.
R6: Access from a non-Trusted Device (e.g., a public computer or a new phone) is limited to 'Read Only' mode for 48 hours.
R1: The BCCC must be conducted via a secure, encrypted video bridge hosted natively on the Sun Chip platform.
R1: No exception can be granted without a 'Risk Impact Score' calculated by the OCRO.
R2: Exceptions are limited to a maximum duration of 7 days (Standard) or 90 days (Executive).
R3: 'Emergency Wire Outside Window' (E-WOW) requests require a notarized 'Manual Wire Indemnity' and a BCCC with the Head of Operations.
R4: All exceptions are logged in the 'Master Exception Registry' and issued a unique Audit Receipt.
R5: A 'Cooling-Off Period' of 2 hours applies to the activation of any approved exception.
Age Group: Ages 16-22, Literacy Focus: Practical Application, Key Activities: Credit management; tax filing basics; managing larger allowances
Age Group: Ages 11-15, Literacy Focus: Financial Awareness, Key Activities: Youth bank accounts; budgeting basics; introduction to investing
Age Group: Ages 5-10, Literacy Focus: Foundational Concepts, Key Activities: Savings jars; understanding needs vs. wants; family money values
IS-BAO Stage: Stage 3, Maturity Level: Full Integration, Definition: Safety culture is sustained and fully integrated into the business
IS-BAO Stage: Stage 2, Maturity Level: Effective Management, Definition: Safety risks are being proactively managed and mitigated
Art Category: Blue-Chip Masters, Typical LTV Ratio: 50%-70%, Rationale: High market stability; global demand
Art Category: Established Contemporary, Typical LTV Ratio: 40%-60%, Rationale: Strong secondary market; some volatility
Art Category: Emerging Artists, Typical LTV Ratio: 20%-40%, Rationale: Niche market; lower liquidity
IS-BAO Stage: Stage 1, Maturity Level: Initial Establishment, Definition: Infrastructure is in place and safety activities are targeted
Age Group: Post-Graduate, Literacy Focus: Stewardship & Leadership, Key Activities: Family office internships; participation as co-trustees; philanthropy
